title: Smart Scheduling: Add/Subtract Days from Master Dates to Populate Task Timelines
excerpt:
Description
We’d love enhanced date logic in Timeline or Date columns that allows for both forward and reverse scheduling. This would significantly improve our ability to plan and track deadlines for event-based projects…

posts.author: MMH (Maryam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

We’d love enhanced date logic in Timeline or Date columns that allows for both forward and reverse scheduling. This would significantly improve our ability to plan and track deadlines for event-based projects.

Desired Functionality:

  1. Forward Scheduling:

Users input a start date.

Users input a duration (e.g., X number of days).

The system automatically calculates and populates a due date by adding the number of days to the start date.

  1. Reverse Scheduling from a Master Event Date:

We designate a master event date (e.g., the date of an event, launch, or final deadline).

Users assign a task-specific estimated duration or lead time (e.g., “start this task 10 days before the event”).

The system calculates and auto-populates a start date for the task by subtracting that lead time from the master event date.

What are you trying to achieve

This functionality would allow us to:

Automatically generate task timelines for repeatable workflows.

Plan backwards from a key milestone without manually calculating dates.

Easily update all timelines when the master date shifts (e.g., event rescheduled).

Bonus if it integrates with automations or formulas to update dynamically when the master date or lead time changes.
